aliases: Steamed rice flour dumpling with coconut filling

english: Steamed rice flour dumpling with coconut filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Tepung Bungkus; closest defensible match is steamed rice cake (FOODON_03315671). Tepung Bungkus is a Malay/Singaporean steamed rice flour dumpling with sweetened coconut filling.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (rice-based snack, steamed) used as closest defensible category. No exact FoodEx2 entry for Tepung Bungkus exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Singaporean/Malay snack item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Tepung Bungkus is approximately 80 g, equivalent to one steamed rice flour dumpling piece as commonly sold at Singaporean hawker stalls and markets.

nutrient_source.title: Tepung Bungkus — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Tepung Bungkus (cr_id F11090063). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Tepung Bungkus found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 83 (high) based on analogous steamed glutinous/rice flour-based Asian confection entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (e.g., rice cakes GI ~82–85, glutinous rice-based snacks GI ~80–86). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (83 × 50.2) / 100 ≈ 41.6, using 80 g serving size (50.2 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and estimated sugar content promoting insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 160 mg/100 g is moderate but the high sugar load (20 g/100 g) with negligible fibre (0.2 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium and fluid retention.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Tepung Bungkus to occasional small portions, as its high refined carbohydrate and sugar content (20 g/100 g sugar, 0.2 g/100 g fibre) promotes insulin-driven sodium retention even though absolute sodium is moderate at 160 mg/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high carbohydrate content (62.7 g/100 g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.2 g/100 g) and estimated sugar of 20 g/100 g, yielding a high glycemic index (~83) and glycemic load (~41.6 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tepung Bungkus is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high carbohydrate load (62.7 g/100 g), negligible fibre (0.2 g/100 g), high estimated sugar (~20 g/100 g), and high GI (~83), which will cause rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; strict avoidance or very small occasional portions with close glucose monitoring are recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with negligible fibre; saturated fat from coconut filling (~2.1 g/100 g) contributes to LDL-raising potential; no beneficial omega-3, polyphenols, or meaningful fibre present.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Tepung Bungkus only occasionally and in small portions, as its high refined carbohydrate content (62.7 g/100 g), high sugar (~20 g/100 g), saturated fat from coconut (~2.1 g/100 g), and negligible fibre (0.2 g/100 g) collectively contribute to dyslipidaemia risk and provide no cardioprotective nutrients.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined flour base with high estimated sugar (~20 g/100 g) and negligible fibre are pro-inflammatory; no anti-inflammatory factors present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Tepung Bungkus as its refined starch base and high sugar content (~20 g/100 g) promote pro-inflammatory mediators, and the food provides no anti-inflammatory factors such as omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, antioxidants, or meaningful dietary fibre.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tepung Bungkus is a traditional Singaporean and Malay steamed rice flour dumpling filled with sweetened grated coconut. The name literally means 'wrapped flour' in Malay, referring to the soft, slightly chewy rice flour outer layer that encases a moist, sweet coconut filling typically flavoured with pandan leaf and palm sugar. The dumpling is steamed rather than fried, giving it a tender, slightly sticky texture with a mild, fragrant sweetness. It is commonly found at Singaporean hawker centres, wet markets, and Malay kueh stalls, and is a popular breakfast or festive snack. Nutritionally, Tepung Bungkus is a high-carbohydrate snack (62.7 g/100 g) with very low dietary fibre (0.2 g/100 g), moderate sodium (160 mg/100 g), low protein (1.7 g/100 g), and low fat (2.5 g/100 g predominantly from coconut). Estimated sugar content is approximately 20 g/100 g from the sweetened coconut filling. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 83 (high), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without strict portion control. It is plant-based, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The food is culturally significant in Malay and Peranakan culinary traditions in Singapore and Malaysia.
common_user_queries: How many calories in tepung bungkus?
What is the GI of tepung bungkus?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11090063) with confidence 0.95. This is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate based on comparable rice flour-based steamed snacks in the University of Sydney GI Database; sugar_g is estimated from the sweetened coconut filling composition. Saturated fat estimated from coconut fat fraction. All other fields are consistent with the HPB source entry.
notes: 1. sugar_g (20.0 g/100 g) is estimated from the sweetened grated coconut filling composition; HPB source does not explicitly report sugar separately. This estimate is used for health suitability assessments. 2. saturated_fat_g (2.1 g/100 g) is estimated from the coconut fat fraction (coconut fat is ~90% saturated); HPB source reports total fat only. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Tepung Bungkus are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0 as this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per food composition logic. 5. GI of 83 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for comparable steamed rice flour-based Asian snacks; no direct GI measurement for Tepung Bungkus was found. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GL = (83 × 50.16) / 100 ≈ 41.6, using 80 g serving size providing 50.16 g carbohydrate.
